Registration still open for Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race

You still have time to enter the Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race before the early registration deadline. Experience the thrill of kayaking, mountain biking, orienteering, and trail running in and around St. Edward State Park in Kenmore.

Take advantage of the early registration fee (just $80/person, which includes kayak rental, maps, a t-shirt, and a picnic lunch) by sending in your entry, post-marked by August 15. After August 15, the registration fee goes up to $100/person. The final registration deadline is August 24. The field is limited to 100 individuals, so enter now. Online registration is also available (processing fees, paid to Active.com, apply).

See the event page on our web site for additional details: www.CascadeOC.org/Degrees/SixHome.html.

New Publicity Coordinator

Welcome to Doug Hathaway, Cascade's new Publicity Coordinator. Doug is a former member of the Husky Orienteering Club and is so new that that's all I know about him. More information about Doug and the rest of the board members will appear in a future issue of Bearing 315. In the meantime, please welcome Doug and thank him profusely for volunteering for this critical position.

WIOL/Winter O' Series to Begin

The 2003 editions of the Washington Interscholastic Orienteering League and the Winter O' Series are set to begin on November 8 at Farrel-McWhirter Park in Redmond.

Both sets of meets take place concurrently. The WIOL courses are for school-age participants from elementary through high school, while the O' Series courses cater to the more "mature" orienteer.

This year, we will be using the SPORTident electronic punching system, which the club recently purchased and used successfully at Pacific North West Orienteering Festival in June. The complete preliminary WIOL/O' Series schedule is:

DateLocation
November 8Farrel-McWhirter Park, Redmond
November 22Marymoor Park, Redmond
December 6Lincoln Park, West Seattle
December 20University of Washington, Seattle
January 10Pt. Defiance Park, Tacoma
January 24Hamlin Park, Shoreline
February 14Woodland Park, Seattle
February 21Championships, Ft. Steilacoom, Tacoma

Check the website for the most up-to-date information.

Ballard Bike-O September 7

The annual Ballard Bike-O will take place on September 7. Patrick Kelly has designed 4 courses to choose from. The beginner course is 3.8k, followed by Course 2, "The Churches of Ballard," on which all controls are churches. Courses 3 and 4 will be "freestyle" courses: visit controls in any order. If you make it to all 19, you've done Course 4. Come back with 12-18 controls, and you've done Course 3. As in past years, rather than punching markers, you will be given questions to answer to prove you visited a control. Meet near the Ballard Community Center on the corner of N.W. 60th St. and 28th Ave. N.W.

Ultimate Orienteer Scoring System
The winner of each event receives 1000 points. Everyone else receives a percentage of 1000 points based on their time compared to that of the winner. For example, if the winner has a time of 25 minutes and your time is 50 minutes, you will receive 500 points. Your best 3 scores are combined for your total score.

Registration still open for Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race

You still have time to enter the Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race before the early registration deadline. Experience the thrill of kayaking, mountain biking, orienteering, and trail running in and around St. Edward State Park in Kenmore.

Take advantage of the early registration fee (just $80/person, which includes kayak rental, maps, a t-shirt, and a picnic lunch) by sending in your entry, post-marked by August 15. After August 15, the registration fee goes up to $100/person. The final registration deadline is August 24. The field is limited to 100 individuals, so enter now. Online registration is also available (processing fees, paid to Active.com, apply).
See the event page on our web site for additional details: www.CascadeOC.org/Degrees/SixHome.html.

Welcome to Doug Hathaway, Cascade's new Publicity Coordinator. Doug is a former member of the Husky Orienteering Club and is so new that that's all I know about him. More information about Doug and the rest of the board members will appear in a future issue of Bearing 315. In the meantime, please welcome Doug and thank him profusely for volunteering for this critical position.
The 2003 editions of the Washington Interscholastic Orienteering League and the Winter O' Series are set to begin on November 8 at Farrel-McWhirter Park in Redmond.
Both sets of meets take place concurrently. The WIOL courses are for school-age participants from elementary through high school, while the O' Series courses cater to the more "mature" orienteer.

This year, we will be using the SPORTident electronic punching system, which the club recently purchased and used successfully at Pacific North West Orienteering Festival in June. The complete preliminary WIOL/O' Series schedule is:
Check the website for the most up-to-date information.

The annual Ballard Bike-O will take place on September 7. Patrick Kelly has designed 4 courses to choose from. The beginner course is 3.8k, followed by Course 2, "The Churches of Ballard," on which all controls are churches. Courses 3 and 4 will be "freestyle" courses: visit controls in any order. If you make it to all 19, you've done Course 4. Come back with 12-18 controls, and you've done Course 3. As in past years, rather than punching markers, you will be given questions to answer to prove you visited a control. Meet near the Ballard Community Center on the corner of N.W. 60th St. and 28th Ave. N.W.

Ultimate Orienteer Scoring System The winner of each event receives 1000 points. Everyone else receives a percentage of 1000 points based on their time compared to that of the winner. For example, if the winner has a time of 25 minutes and your time is 50 minutes, you will receive 500 points. Your best 3 scores are combined for your total score.
